question: Is the text "RIVERSIDE TEA HOUSE: Sit a while and speak slowly, no rush even when the tea grows cold" spelled correctly, including the colon?
Is the text "RIVERSIDE TEA HOUSE..." displayed on a wooden sign at the tea house entrance using bold serif lettering?
Is the text "Tonight brings wind and moonlight, safe travels on your way" spelled correctly?
Is the text "Tonight brings wind..." displayed on red paper lanterns using elegant cursive script?
Is the verse "Sunset fades behind the hills, rivers flow to distant seas, to gaze upon a thousand miles, climb higher still with grace and ease" spelled correctly, including all commas?
Is the verse "Sunset fades..." displayed on a long rice paper scroll hanging under the corridor in brush calligraphy style?
Is the text "Walk until the waters end, then sit and watch the clouds arise" spelled correctly?
Is the text "Walk until the waters end..." displayed on an opened paper fan in delicate italic script?
Is the text "LONG ROADS FEAR NOT SLOWNESS, ONLY STOPPING; WHEN TROUBLED, FIRST DRINK TEA AND THEN SPEAK" spelled correctly, including the semicolon and all-caps format?
Is the text "LONG ROADS FEAR NOT SLOWNESS..." displayed on a stone stele at the bridge head in carved Roman capitals?
Is the text "CLEAR WIND BRIDGE: Cross with care and mind the steps below" spelled correctly, including the colon?
Is the text "CLEAR WIND BRIDGE..." displayed on a bridge plaque in gold-leaf serif lettering?
Is the text "Accounts: 3 coins..." displayed on a counter ledger in a printed typeface?
Is the text "Farewell traveler, may your journey be safe, and good tidings always find you" spelled correctly?
Is the text "Farewell traveler..." displayed on the river boat sail in bold brush lettering?
Is the image rendered in an ukiyo-e woodblock print style (flat color blocks, wave patterns, non-realistic)?
Are the tea house, bridge, and small boat all clearly visible in the scene?
Are all text elements clearly legible without significant blur, missing strokes, or garbled characters?
Does the text perspective align naturally with the surfaces (e.g., curving on the sail/lanterns, angled on the bridge/stele) rather than floating flatly?
